extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Adam attacked at 6.1km to go on Jebel Hafeet, won the stage by 10 seconds over EVENEPOEL, but fell 1 second short of the overall podium.
tactical_patterns: UAE used HESSMANN as domestique to deliver water to rivals mid-climb, controlling without attacking—unconventional resource denial tactics.
YATES attacked 6.1km out when breakaway remnants still existed, using traffic as psychological barrier rather than attacking on open road.
PLAPP defended podium by 1 second over YATES despite cracking, suggesting GC defense pacing calculations more accurate than aggressive climbing.
AG2R planted HANNAN in break as strategic marker for BOUCHARD's late surge to fourth—deliberate positioning paid off.

rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: explosive on steeper ramps of Hafeet but mistimed GC ambition—attacked for stage not seconds, revealing priority confusion
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: drove tempo with Yates but couldn't match final kick, defended GC conservatively rather than gambling for stage
rider_signals.PLAPP Lucas: positive: clung to podium by 1 second through precise pacing despite cracking—calculated defense superior to Yates' aggressive gamble
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: negative: lost podium by 3 seconds after looking over shoulder repeatedly—psychological weakness cost measurable time
rider_signals.BOUCHARD Geoffrey: positive: AG2R's breakaway marker strategy delivered fourth place, showing French team's tactical maturity improved
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: negative: pre-stage favorite struggled and finished elsewhere, suggesting form collapse or misjudged heat preparation
